autumn-leaves.name: Autumn Leaves
autumn-leaves.description: Ground covered in fallen orange, red, and yellow leaves over dark soil. Place in deciduous forest areas during autumn theming. Use in clusters of 5-15 tiles beneath tree props. Pairs well with forest-rotting-leaves and forest-deadwood.

beach-coarse.name: Coarse Beach Sand
beach-coarse.description: Rough, grainy sand with visible pebbles. Use as transition between fine beach sand and rocky/grassy areas. Place in strips 2-4 tiles wide along the inland edge of beaches.

beach-cracked.name: Cracked Beach
beach-cracked.description: Dried, cracked sandy surface. Use sparingly in upper beach areas that are far from water and baked by sun. Good for desolate coastal areas. Cluster 3-8 tiles.

beach-footprints.name: Beach Footprints
beach-footprints.description: Sandy beach with visible footprints. Use very sparingly (1-3 tiles) near NPC spawn points or paths leading to/from the beach to suggest foot traffic.

beach-golden.name: Golden Beach Sand
beach-golden.description: Warm golden sand, slightly darker than standard beach-sand. Use as base fill for tropical or sun-drenched beach areas. Large patches of 20-50 tiles.

beach-pebbles.name: Pebble Beach
beach-pebbles.description: Beach covered in small smooth pebbles. Use along rocky coastlines or as transition from beach to rocky terrain. Strips of 3-8 tiles wide.

beach-sand.name: Beach Sand
beach-sand.description: Standard pale tan beach sand. The primary base-fill tile for beach biomes. Use in large areas 30+ tiles for main beach expanses. Should border water on one side and transition to grass/dirt on the other.

beach-seashells.name: Seashell Beach
beach-seashells.description: Sand with scattered seashells. Use as accent near the waterline, 1-4 tiles at a time. Adds visual interest to beach areas.

beach-shoreline.name: Beach Shoreline
beach-shoreline.description: Wet sand at the water's edge with a darker, damp appearance. Use in a 1-2 tile strip directly between dry beach sand and water tiles. Critical transition tile.

beach-wet.name: Wet Beach Sand
beach-wet.description: Dark, waterlogged sand. Use as transition between shoreline and dry beach, 1-3 tiles wide. Slightly darker than shoreline.

beach-white.name: White Beach Sand
beach-white.description: Bright white tropical sand. Use for pristine tropical beaches. Can be mixed with beach-golden for variation. Large patches 20+ tiles.

carpet-blue.name: Blue Carpet
carpet-blue.description: Rich blue woven carpet with decorative pattern. Use inside buildings for noble or magical rooms. Place in rectangular areas matching room dimensions. 4-20 tiles per room.

carpet-green.name: Green Carpet
carpet-green.description: Green woven carpet with decorative pattern. Use inside buildings for studies, libraries, or merchant halls. Rectangular areas matching room shape.

carpet-purple.name: Purple Carpet
carpet-purple.description: Royal purple woven carpet. Use for throne rooms, temples, or wizard towers. Rare and prestigious. Small rectangular areas 4-12 tiles.

carpet-red.name: Red Carpet
carpet-red.description: Ornate red carpet with gold trim pattern. Use for grand halls, throne rooms, or as a runner leading to important areas. Can be placed as a 1-2 tile wide strip for hallway runners.

clover-patch.name: Clover Patch
clover-patch.description: Dense green clover with small purple flowers mixed into grass. Use as accent patches in meadows and fields, 3-8 tiles. Adds variety to large grass areas.

cobblestone-path.name: Cobblestone Path
cobblestone-path.description: Rounded cobblestones set in mortar, lighter gray color. Use for village paths, garden walkways, and secondary roads. Strips 1-3 tiles wide connecting buildings or areas. Less formal than cobblestone-road.

cobblestone-road.name: Cobblestone Road
cobblestone-road.description: Dark blue-gray cobblestones in a formal pattern. Use for main roads through towns and cities, 3-5 tiles wide. The primary road tile for settlements.

cracked-ancient-stone.name: Cracked Ancient Stone
cracked-ancient-stone.description: Weathered stone with deep cracks and an aged appearance. Use for ruins, ancient temples, and abandoned structures. Clusters of 5-20 tiles forming remnants of old buildings.

cracked-desert-earth.name: Cracked Desert Earth
cracked-desert-earth.description: Dry, deeply cracked earth from extreme heat. Use in arid areas and dried lakebeds. Large patches 10-30 tiles in flat desert areas.

dark-garden-soil.name: Dark Garden Soil
dark-garden-soil.description: Rich, dark tilled soil for gardens and farmland. Use in rectangular plots 3x4 to 6x8 tiles near houses or villages for crop fields and gardens.

dark-green-forest-grass.name: Dark Forest Grass
dark-green-forest-grass.description: Very dark green grass found under dense forest canopy. Use as base-fill for deep forest areas where tree props are placed overhead. Large patches 20+ tiles.

dark-slate.name: Dark Slate
dark-slate.description: Dark gray-blue slate stone surface. Use for dungeon floors, cave areas, and fortress interiors. Can fill large areas 10-30 tiles.

dark-wood-indoor.name: Dark Wood Indoor
dark-wood-indoor.description: Dark stained wooden floorboards. Use inside buildings for taverns, houses, and shops. Fill entire room interiors. The most common indoor floor tile.

desert-adobe.name: Desert Adobe
desert-adobe.description: Tan adobe brick surface. Use for desert building floors and desert town plazas. Rectangular areas 6-20 tiles in desert settlements.

desert-canyon.name: Desert Canyon
desert-canyon.description: Red-orange layered rock forming canyon walls and floors. Use for canyon areas and rocky desert passages. Elongated strips 3-5 tiles wide.

desert-dry-riverbed.name: Desert Dry Riverbed
desert-dry-riverbed.description: Cracked, pale earth of a dried-up river. Use as winding strips 2-4 tiles wide cutting through desert terrain to show ancient water paths.

desert-dunes.name: Desert Dunes
desert-dunes.description: Wavy orange-tan sand dunes with visible ripple patterns. Use in open desert areas as base-fill for sandy expanses. Large patches 20-50 tiles.

desert-dust-trail.name: Desert Dust Trail
desert-dust-trail.description: Faint dusty trail through desert. Use as paths connecting desert settlements or oases, 1-2 tiles wide winding through sand.

desert-gravel.name: Desert Gravel
desert-gravel.description: Coarse desert gravel and small stones. Use as transition between sandy and rocky desert areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

desert-mesa.name: Desert Mesa
desert-mesa.description: Flat-topped red-brown mesa stone. Use for elevated desert plateaus and mesa tops. Clusters of 10-25 tiles forming flat rock surfaces.

desert-oasis.name: Desert Oasis
desert-oasis.description: Lush green vegetation around a water source in the desert. Very rare — use only 3-8 tiles at oasis locations surrounded by desert sand. Always pair with water tiles nearby.

desert-red-sand.name: Desert Red Sand
desert-red-sand.description: Deep red-orange desert sand. Use for Red Desert biome areas, large patches 15-40 tiles. Visually distinct from standard yellow desert-sand.

desert-rock.name: Desert Rock
desert-rock.description: Exposed rocky desert surface. Use for rocky outcrops and barren desert terrain. Irregular clusters of 5-15 tiles breaking up sandy areas.

desert-salt-flat.name: Desert Salt Flat
desert-salt-flat.description: Bright white crystalline salt flat surface. Use for dried salt lake areas. Large flat expanses 20-50 tiles. Visually striking and desolate.

desert-sand.name: Desert Sand
desert-sand.description: Standard pale tan desert sand. The primary base-fill tile for desert biomes. Use in very large areas 50+ tiles. The default ground for any desert zone.

desert-sandstone.name: Desert Sandstone
desert-sandstone.description: Carved sandstone blocks. Use for desert building floors, temple foundations, and ancient ruins in desert areas. Rectangular patches 6-20 tiles.

desert-scorched.name: Desert Scorched Earth
desert-scorched.description: Heat-scorched golden desert surface with visible cracks. Use near volcanic/desert borders or extremely hot zones. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

desert-scrub.name: Desert Scrub
desert-scrub.description: Desert sand with sparse scrubby vegetation. Use as transition between barren desert and greener areas. Scattered patches of 5-15 tiles at desert edges.

dirt-chalky.name: Chalky Dirt
dirt-chalky.description: Pale chalky-white dirt. Use near limestone cliffs or chalk deposits. Small patches of 3-10 tiles as accent.

dirt-clay.name: Clay Dirt
dirt-clay.description: Reddish-brown clay soil. Use near rivers, in pottery village areas, or as transition terrain. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

dirt-drought.name: Drought Dirt
dirt-drought.description: Extremely dry, parched dirt with deep cracks. Use in drought-stricken temperate areas or desert edges. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

dirt-dry-cracked.name: Dry Cracked Dirt
dirt-dry-cracked.description: Hard dirt with visible cracks from dryness. Use in arid zones and dried areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles near desert borders.

dirt-grass-sprouts.name: Dirt with Grass Sprouts
dirt-grass-sprouts.description: Brown dirt with new grass starting to grow. Excellent transition tile between dirt paths and grass areas. Use in 1-3 tile wide borders.

dirt-gravelly.name: Gravelly Dirt
dirt-gravelly.description: Dirt mixed with small gravel and stones. Use near roads, construction areas, and rocky terrain. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

dirt-gray.name: Gray Dirt
dirt-gray.description: Ashen gray dirt. Use near volcanic areas, burned zones, or mines. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

dirt-hardpan.name: Hardpan Dirt
dirt-hardpan.description: Very hard-packed, compressed dirt surface. Use for well-traveled areas without formal roads. Patches of 8-20 tiles near settlements.

dirt-loamy.name: Loamy Dirt
dirt-loamy.description: Rich dark loamy soil. Use for farmland and fertile areas near water. Rectangular plots 4-16 tiles for crop fields.

dirt-loose.name: Loose Dirt
dirt-loose.description: Soft, freshly disturbed loose dirt. Use for excavation sites, fresh graves, or construction zones. Small patches 2-8 tiles.

dirt-mound.name: Dirt Mound
dirt-mound.description: Raised dirt mound or hill. Use as accent in open fields, 1-3 tiles at a time. Suggests uneven terrain.

dirt-packed.name: Packed Dirt
dirt-packed.description: Hard-packed brown dirt surface. Use as base-fill for camps, clearings, and unpaved village squares. Large areas 15-40 tiles.

dirt-path.name: Dirt Path
dirt-path.description: Well-worn brown dirt path. Use for trails through wilderness, 1-2 tiles wide connecting areas. The most common natural path tile.

dirt-pebbly.name: Pebbly Dirt
dirt-pebbly.description: Dirt with scattered small pebbles. Use as transition between dirt and rocky areas. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

dirt-rocky.name: Rocky Dirt
dirt-rocky.description: Dirt with larger embedded rocks. Use near hills, mountains, and rocky outcrops. Patches of 5-15 tiles as transition to stone.

dirt-roots.name: Dirt with Roots
dirt-roots.description: Dirt with visible tree roots criss-crossing the surface. Use under and around tree props in forests. Patches of 3-8 tiles near large trees.

dirt-sandy.name: Sandy Dirt
dirt-sandy.description: Light brown dirt mixed with sand. Use as transition between desert/beach and temperate zones. Strips 2-5 tiles wide.

dirt-smooth.name: Smooth Dirt
dirt-smooth.description: Smooth, even brown dirt surface. Use for indoor dirt floors in rustic buildings, or flat outdoor clearings. Areas of 8-25 tiles.

dirt-topsoil.name: Topsoil
dirt-topsoil.description: Dark, nutrient-rich topsoil. Use for garden beds, farmland, and fertile areas. Rectangular patches near settlements.

dirt-wet.name: Wet Dirt
dirt-wet.description: Dark, waterlogged dirt. Use near rivers, lakes, and in rainy areas. Strips 2-4 tiles wide along waterways.

dry-autumn-grass.name: Dry Autumn Grass
dry-autumn-grass.description: Golden-yellow dried grass from late autumn. Use in seasonal transition areas and dry meadows. Large patches 15-40 tiles for autumn-themed zones.

dusty-worn-road.name: Dusty Worn Road
dusty-worn-road.description: Heavily used, dusty road surface. Use for major trade routes and well-traveled roads between towns, 3-5 tiles wide.

fern-undergrowth.name: Fern Undergrowth
fern-undergrowth.description: Dense fern ground cover in dark green. Use under forest canopy in moist areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles mixed with dark-green-forest-grass.

flagstone-tiles.name: Flagstone Tiles
flagstone-tiles.description: Purple-gray flat flagstone paving. Use for plazas, courtyards, and formal outdoor areas. Large rectangular areas 10-30 tiles.

floor-brick.name: Brick Floor
floor-brick.description: Red brick floor pattern. Use for kitchens, forges, and rustic indoor areas. Fill room interiors.

floor-cellar-dirt.name: Cellar Dirt Floor
floor-cellar-dirt.description: Rough dirt floor for cellars and basements. Use for underground rooms and rustic storage areas.

floor-checkered.name: Checkered Floor
floor-checkered.description: Black and white checkered tile floor. Use for grand halls, ballrooms, and formal indoor spaces.

floor-dark-oak.name: Dark Oak Floor
floor-dark-oak.description: Rich dark oak wood planks with warm reddish-brown tone. Use for upscale taverns, noble houses, and guild halls.

floor-flagstone.name: Flagstone Floor
floor-flagstone.description: Indoor flagstone floor with regular cut stones. Use for castle interiors, temples, and formal buildings.

floor-hex-stone.name: Hexagonal Stone Floor
floor-hex-stone.description: Hexagonal stone tile pattern. Use for unique indoor areas like alchemy labs, wizard towers, or ancient structures.

floor-marble.name: Marble Floor
floor-marble.description: White marble floor with gray veining. Use for palaces, temples, and wealthy buildings. Luxurious appearance.

floor-mosaic.name: Mosaic Floor
floor-mosaic.description: Colorful mosaic tile pattern. Use for temples, bathhouses, and artistic indoor spaces. Small accent areas 4-12 tiles.

floor-ornate-gold.name: Ornate Gold Floor
floor-ornate-gold.description: Elaborate gold-trimmed floor tiles. Use very sparingly for throne rooms and treasure vaults. 4-8 tiles maximum.

floor-parquet.name: Parquet Floor
floor-parquet.description: Geometric parquet wood flooring. Use for upper-class residences and formal rooms.

floor-stone-slate.name: Stone Slate Floor
floor-stone-slate.description: Cut slate stone floor. Use for castle corridors, guard rooms, and fortified interiors.

floor-straw-mat.name: Straw Mat Floor
floor-straw-mat.description: Woven straw mat flooring. Use for peasant houses, stables, and rustic interiors. Simple and poor.

floor-terracotta.name: Terracotta Floor
floor-terracotta.description: Warm orange-red terracotta tiles. Use for Mediterranean-style buildings, desert interiors, and kitchens.

floor-wood-plank.name: Wood Plank Floor
floor-wood-plank.description: Simple wooden plank flooring. Use for common houses, shops, and basic indoor areas. The most basic indoor wood floor.

floor-worn-wood.name: Worn Wood Floor
floor-worn-wood.description: Old, worn wooden floor showing age and use. Use for abandoned buildings, old taverns, and rundown interiors.

flower-garden-roses.name: Rose Garden
flower-garden-roses.description: Garden plot with red roses. Use in small decorative patches 2-6 tiles near houses, castles, and parks.

forest-acorns.name: Forest Acorns
forest-acorns.description: Forest floor scattered with acorns and fallen nuts. Use under oak tree props. Sparse accent, 2-5 tiles near specific trees.

forest-birch-leaves.name: Forest Birch Leaves
forest-birch-leaves.description: Light-colored fallen birch leaves on forest floor. Use under birch tree areas. Patches of 3-8 tiles.

forest-bramble.name: Forest Bramble
forest-bramble.description: Thorny bramble bushes on forest floor. Use to create natural barriers and wild forest edges. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

forest-clearing.name: Forest Clearing
forest-clearing.description: Bright green grass in a forest clearing where sunlight reaches. Use for open areas within dense forest, 8-20 tiles forming circular clearings.

forest-deadwood.name: Forest Deadwood
forest-deadwood.description: Dead branches and twigs covering the forest floor. Use in older or decaying forest areas. Patches of 3-8 tiles.

forest-glade.name: Forest Glade
forest-glade.description: Lush green glade with soft grass and scattered flowers. Use for magical or serene forest locations. Small special areas 6-15 tiles.

forest-ivy.name: Forest Ivy
forest-ivy.description: Ground-covering ivy in deep green. Use near ruins, old walls, and ancient forest structures. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

forest-lichen-rock.name: Forest Lichen Rock
forest-lichen-rock.description: Rocky ground covered in colorful lichen. Use for rocky forest areas and boulder fields. Patches of 3-8 tiles.

forest-loam.name: Forest Loam
forest-loam.description: Rich, dark forest floor soil. Use as base-fill for deep forest interiors. Large patches 15-30 tiles beneath tree canopy.

forest-path.name: Forest Path
forest-path.description: Narrow dirt trail through forest. Use as 1-2 tile wide paths winding through forest areas connecting clearings.

forest-roots.name: Forest Roots
forest-roots.description: Thick exposed tree roots across the ground. Use at the base of large tree props. 1-4 tiles directly adjacent to trees.

forest-rotting-leaves.name: Forest Rotting Leaves
forest-rotting-leaves.description: Decomposing brown leaves on the forest floor. Use in old-growth forest and autumn areas. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

forest-stream-bed.name: Forest Stream Bed
forest-stream-bed.description: Smooth stones and pebbles of a forest stream bed. Use as 1-3 tile wide strips for small streams through forests.

forest-stumps.name: Forest Stumps
forest-stumps.description: Area with old tree stumps from logging. Use for logging camps and cleared forest edges. Patches of 3-8 tiles.

forest-undergrowth.name: Forest Undergrowth
forest-undergrowth.description: Thick low bushes and small plants covering the forest floor. Use in dense forest areas. Large patches 10-25 tiles.

garden-sprouts.name: Garden Sprouts
garden-sprouts.description: Small green seedlings growing in tilled soil. Use for farm plots and gardens near villages. Rectangular patches 3-12 tiles.

grass.name: Standard Grass
grass.description: Default green grass with visible blades. THE most common terrain tile — use as the primary base-fill for all temperate zones. Hundreds of tiles per chunk for open fields and meadows.

grass-bleached.name: Bleached Grass
grass-bleached.description: Sun-bleached pale grass. Use for drought areas or hot summer zones. Patches of 10-30 tiles.

grass-blue-green.name: Blue-Green Grass
grass-blue-green.description: Cool blue-green tinted grass. Use near water sources and in misty/cool areas. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

grass-clover.name: Clover Grass
grass-clover.description: Grass with abundant clover mixed in. Use in meadows and pastoral areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles near clover-patch.

grass-dewy.name: Dewy Grass
grass-dewy.description: Grass glistening with morning dew. Use in low-lying areas near water. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

grass-dirt-edge.name: Grass-Dirt Edge
grass-dirt-edge.description: Half grass, half dirt — a key transition tile. Use as 1-tile border between grass areas and dirt paths/clearings.

grass-emerald.name: Emerald Grass
grass-emerald.description: Vibrant deep emerald green grass. Use for lush, well-watered areas and magical glades. Patches of 10-25 tiles.

grass-eroded.name: Eroded Grass
grass-eroded.description: Thin grass on eroded ground showing dirt underneath. Use on hillsides and slopes. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

grass-golden.name: Golden Grass
grass-golden.description: Golden-yellow grass suggesting late summer or savanna. Use for warm-toned open areas. Large patches 15-40 tiles.

grass-highland.name: Highland Grass
grass-highland.description: Tough, windswept highland grass. Use for elevated moor and hill areas. Large patches 15-40 tiles.

grass-mossy.name: Mossy Grass
grass-mossy.description: Grass with patches of green moss. Use in damp, shaded areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles near water or under trees.

grass-new-growth.name: New Growth Grass
grass-new-growth.description: Fresh, bright green new grass growth. Use for spring-themed areas and recently cleared land. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

grass-prairie.name: Prairie Grass
grass-prairie.description: Tall prairie grass blowing in the wind. Use for open plains and grassland areas. Very large patches 30+ tiles.

grass-purple-lavender.name: Lavender Grass
grass-purple-lavender.description: Grass with purple lavender flowers. Use as accent in meadows and near settlements. Patches of 3-10 tiles for visual variety.

grass-red-poppies.name: Poppy Grass
grass-red-poppies.description: Grass dotted with red poppies. Use as accent in open fields. Scattered patches of 2-8 tiles.

grass-rocky.name: Rocky Grass
grass-rocky.description: Grass growing between rocks and stones. Use as transition from grassy areas to rocky terrain. Strips 2-4 tiles.

grass-sandy.name: Sandy Grass
grass-sandy.description: Sparse grass growing in sandy soil. Key transition between beach/desert and grass biomes. Strips 2-5 tiles wide.

grass-savanna.name: Savanna Grass
grass-savanna.description: Dry, golden-tan savanna grass. Use for African-style savanna biomes. Very large patches 40+ tiles.

grass-sparse.name: Sparse Grass
grass-sparse.description: Thin, sparse grass with visible dirt. Use as transition between grass and barren areas. Strips 2-5 tiles.

grass-standard.name: Standard Grass Alt
grass-standard.description: Alternative standard grass tile. Mix with main grass tile for variation in large temperate areas. Base-fill usage.

grass-thick.name: Thick Grass
grass-thick.description: Dense, tall grass. Use for well-watered meadows and unmowed fields. Patches of 10-30 tiles.

grass-tufts.name: Grass Tufts
grass-tufts.description: Scattered tufts of grass on bare ground. Use as transition from grass to dirt or stone. 1-3 tile border strips.

grass-white-daisies.name: Daisy Grass
grass-white-daisies.description: Grass with white daisies scattered about. Use as meadow accent. Patches of 3-8 tiles.

grass-worn.name: Worn Grass
grass-worn.description: Trampled, worn-down grass showing heavy foot traffic. Use around paths, near buildings, and in high-traffic areas. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

grass-yellow-wildflowers.name: Yellow Wildflower Grass
grass-yellow-wildflowers.description: Grass with bright yellow wildflowers. Use as meadow accent. Patches of 3-8 tiles for color variety.

gravel-path.name: Gravel Path
gravel-path.description: Loose gravel path surface. Use for garden paths, driveways, and informal walkways. Strips 1-3 tiles wide.

light-spring-meadow-grass.name: Spring Meadow Grass
light-spring-meadow-grass.description: Bright light-green spring grass. Use for fresh spring meadows and sunlit fields. Large patches 15-40 tiles. Lighter than standard grass.

lush-green-grass.name: Lush Green Grass
lush-green-grass.description: Very green, well-watered lush grass. Use near rivers and in fertile valleys. Large patches 15-30 tiles.

mixed-wildflower-meadow.name: Wildflower Meadow
mixed-wildflower-meadow.description: Colorful mix of wildflowers in grass — red, yellow, white, purple. Use as meadow centerpiece. Patches of 8-20 tiles in open sunny areas.

mossy-cobblestone.name: Mossy Cobblestone
mossy-cobblestone.description: Old cobblestones overgrown with green moss. Use for abandoned roads, ancient ruins, and overgrown settlements. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

mossy-ground.name: Mossy Ground
mossy-ground.description: Ground thickly covered in green moss. Use in damp forest areas, cave entrances, and near waterfalls. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

muddy-trail.name: Muddy Trail
muddy-trail.description: Dark, muddy path. Use for rain-soaked trails and swamp edges. Strips 1-3 tiles wide.

mushroom-forest.name: Mushroom Forest Floor
mushroom-forest.description: Forest floor dotted with red mushrooms. Use for magical or old-growth forest areas. Sparse accent, patches of 3-8 tiles.

overgrown-weedy-grass.name: Overgrown Weedy Grass
overgrown-weedy-grass.description: Tall, unkempt grass with weeds. Use for abandoned areas, ruins, and neglected land. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

packed-dirt-road.name: Packed Dirt Road
packed-dirt-road.description: Hard-packed brown dirt road. Use for main routes between villages, 3-5 tiles wide. The standard rural road tile.

patchy-grass-dirt.name: Patchy Grass-Dirt
patchy-grass-dirt.description: Mixed patches of grass and exposed dirt. THE key transition tile between grass and dirt zones. Use in 1-3 tile borders everywhere these biomes meet.

pine-needles.name: Pine Needles
pine-needles.description: Reddish-brown carpet of fallen pine needles. Use under pine/conifer tree props. Large patches 10-25 tiles in coniferous forest zones.

pl-grass-1.name: PixelLab Grass 1
pl-grass-1.description: Light green pixel art grass variant. Mix with other grass tiles for variation. Base-fill usage in temperate zones.

pl-grass-2.name: PixelLab Grass 2
pl-grass-2.description: Medium green pixel art grass variant. Mix with other grass tiles for variation. Base-fill usage in temperate zones.

pl-grass-3.name: PixelLab Grass 3
pl-grass-3.description: Dark green pixel art grass variant with purple tints. Mix with other grass tiles for variation. Base-fill usage.

red-brick-road.name: Red Brick Road
red-brick-road.description: Red brick paved road surface. Use for wealthy town districts and main plazas, 3-5 tiles wide. Upscale alternative to cobblestone.

red-clay-ground.name: Red Clay Ground
red-clay-ground.description: Bright red clay earth. Use in tropical/subtropical areas and near clay deposits. Patches of 10-25 tiles.

rocks-pebbles.name: Rocks and Pebbles
rocks-pebbles.description: Ground covered in loose rocks and pebbles. Use for rocky terrain, riverbeds, and mountain base. Patches of 5-20 tiles.

rough-hewn-stone.name: Rough Hewn Stone
rough-hewn-stone.description: Roughly cut stone surface. Use for castle walls, mountain outcrops, and fortress foundations. Patches of 5-20 tiles.

rug-fur-hide.name: Fur Hide Rug
rug-fur-hide.description: Animal fur/hide rug. Use as 1-3 tile accent in hunter cabins, taverns, or barbarian dwellings.

sandy-path.name: Sandy Path
sandy-path.description: Path through sandy terrain. Use as trails leading to/from beaches, 1-2 tiles wide.

shallow-water.name: Shallow Water
shallow-water.description: Clear shallow water showing the bottom. Use as 1-3 tile border around deeper water bodies. Critical transition between land and water.

short-trimmed-lawn.name: Short Trimmed Lawn
short-trimmed-lawn.description: Neatly trimmed short grass. Use around wealthy houses, castles, and formal gardens. Rectangular patches 8-25 tiles.

snow-deep.name: Deep Snow
snow-deep.description: Thick deep snow cover. Use in heavy snowfall areas and drifts. Large patches 15-40 tiles.

snow-drift.name: Snow Drift
snow-drift.description: Wind-blown snow forming drifts. Use in exposed areas with wind patterns. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

snow-frost-crystal.name: Frost Crystal Snow
snow-frost-crystal.description: Snow with visible frost crystal patterns. Use for magical winter areas or extreme cold zones. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

snow-frost-grass.name: Frost-Covered Grass
snow-frost-grass.description: Grass with a light frost coating. Key transition between snow and temperate zones. Strips 2-5 tiles wide at biome borders.

snow-frozen-earth.name: Frozen Earth
snow-frozen-earth.description: Hard frozen dirt with frost. Use as base ground in snow biome where snow is thin. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

snow-frozen-lake.name: Frozen Lake
snow-frozen-lake.description: Frozen lake surface with visible ice cracks. Use for frozen bodies of water. Fill lake areas 10-40 tiles. Walkable unlike regular water.

snow-frozen-mud.name: Frozen Mud
snow-frozen-mud.description: Mud frozen solid with frost. Use near frozen water and in thawing areas. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

snow-glacier.name: Glacier
snow-glacier.description: Dense glacial ice, blue-white. Use for glacier areas and permanent ice fields. Large patches 15-40 tiles.

snow-ground.name: Snow Ground
snow-ground.description: Standard white snow-covered ground. THE primary base-fill for snow biomes. Use in very large areas 50+ tiles. Default snow terrain.

snow-ice-sheet.name: Ice Sheet
snow-ice-sheet.description: Smooth ice sheet surface. Use for frozen rivers and icy paths. Strips and patches of 5-20 tiles.

snow-icy-rock.name: Icy Rock
snow-icy-rock.description: Rocky surface coated in ice. Use for mountain peaks and frozen rock outcrops. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

snow-path.name: Snow Path
snow-path.description: Trampled snow forming a path. Use for trails through snow zones, 1-2 tiles wide.

snow-permafrost.name: Permafrost
snow-permafrost.description: Permanently frozen ground with a dark, hard surface. Use in tundra areas. Large patches 15-30 tiles.

snow-pine-needles.name: Snowy Pine Needles
snow-pine-needles.description: Pine needles on snow. Use under conifer trees in snow biome. Patches of 3-10 tiles near trees.

snow-slush.name: Snow Slush
snow-slush.description: Melting, slushy snow. Use at the edge of snow biomes and in thawing areas. Strips 2-4 tiles as transition.

snow-tundra-moss.name: Tundra Moss
snow-tundra-moss.description: Low-growing tundra moss on frozen ground. Use for arctic tundra biome. Patches of 5-15 tiles mixed with permafrost.

stone-dungeon.name: Dungeon Stone
stone-dungeon.description: Dark gray dungeon floor stone. Use for underground dungeons, caves, and prison areas. Large areas 15-40 tiles.

stone-plaza.name: Stone Plaza
stone-plaza.description: Cut stone paving for plazas and town squares. Use for village centers and gathering areas. Large areas 15-40 tiles.

straw-hay.name: Straw Hay
straw-hay.description: Scattered straw and hay on the ground. Use for stable floors, barns, and farm areas. Patches of 4-12 tiles inside farm buildings.

swamp-algae.name: Swamp Algae
swamp-algae.description: Stagnant water covered in green algae. Use for swamp pools and bogs. Patches of 5-15 tiles. Not walkable.

swamp-bog-moss.name: Swamp Bog Moss
swamp-bog-moss.description: Thick spongy moss growing on boggy ground. Use in swamp interiors. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

swamp-cattails.name: Swamp Cattails
swamp-cattails.description: Tall cattail reeds growing in wet soil. Use at edges of swamp water. Strips 1-3 tiles wide around water.

swamp-cypress-roots.name: Swamp Cypress Roots
swamp-cypress-roots.description: Exposed cypress tree roots in swamp water. Use under tree props in swamp areas. 2-5 tiles near trees.

swamp-damp-earth.name: Swamp Damp Earth
swamp-damp-earth.description: Dark, waterlogged earth. Use as base-fill for walkable swamp areas. Large patches 15-30 tiles.

swamp-dead-leaves.name: Swamp Dead Leaves
swamp-dead-leaves.description: Decaying leaves floating on wet ground. Use in swamp areas. Patches of 3-10 tiles.

swamp-fungus.name: Swamp Fungus
swamp-fungus.description: Ground covered in swamp mushrooms and fungal growth. Use sparingly for mysterious or dangerous swamp spots. 2-6 tiles.

swamp-grass.name: Swamp Grass
swamp-grass.description: Dark, coarse grass growing in swampy conditions. Use as transition between swamp and normal terrain. Strips 3-6 tiles wide.

swamp-lily-pads.name: Swamp Lily Pads
swamp-lily-pads.description: Dark water with green lily pads floating on top. Use for swamp ponds and still water. Patches of 5-15 tiles. Not walkable.

swamp-mangrove.name: Swamp Mangrove
swamp-mangrove.description: Tangled mangrove root system above waterlogged ground. Use near coastal swamps. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

swamp-marsh-reeds.name: Swamp Marsh Reeds
swamp-marsh-reeds.description: Tall marsh reeds in wet soil. Use along swamp water edges. Strips 1-3 tiles wide.

swamp-mud.name: Swamp Mud
swamp-mud.description: Dark, thick swamp mud. Use as base-fill for swamp terrain. Large patches 15-40 tiles.

swamp-peat.name: Swamp Peat
swamp-peat.description: Dark brown peat bog surface. Use for deep boggy areas. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

swamp-quicksand.name: Swamp Quicksand
swamp-quicksand.description: Dangerous quicksand with a deceptively smooth surface. Use very sparingly (1-4 tiles) as hazards in swamps. Not walkable.

swamp-shallows.name: Swamp Shallows
swamp-shallows.description: Shallow murky swamp water. Use as transition between land and deeper swamp water. Strips 1-3 tiles. Not walkable.

tall-wild-grass.name: Tall Wild Grass
tall-wild-grass.description: Very tall wild grass. Use for untamed meadows, field borders, and wild areas. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

volcanic-ash.name: Volcanic Ash
volcanic-ash.description: Gray volcanic ash covering the ground. Use as primary base-fill for volcanic biome outskirts. Large patches 20-50 tiles.

volcanic-basalt.name: Volcanic Basalt
volcanic-basalt.description: Dark basalt rock from cooled lava. Use for volcanic rock formations. Patches of 10-25 tiles.

volcanic-black-sand.name: Volcanic Black Sand
volcanic-black-sand.description: Dark black volcanic sand. Use for volcanic beaches and ash plains. Large patches 15-30 tiles.

volcanic-charred.name: Volcanic Charred Ground
volcanic-charred.description: Blackened, charred earth from volcanic activity. Use near active volcanic areas. Patches of 8-20 tiles.

volcanic-cooled-lava.name: Cooled Lava
volcanic-cooled-lava.description: Solidified, cooled lava with rough dark surface. Use for old lava flows. Strips and patches 5-20 tiles forming flow patterns.

volcanic-embers.name: Volcanic Embers
volcanic-embers.description: Ground with glowing orange embers and sparks. Use near active lava sources. Very small patches 2-6 tiles near lava. Dangerous-looking.

volcanic-fire-crack.name: Volcanic Fire Crack
volcanic-fire-crack.description: Ground with glowing red cracks of fire beneath. Use as accent near volcanic vents. Sparse, 1-4 tiles.

volcanic-lava.name: Volcanic Lava
volcanic-lava.description: Bright red-orange flowing lava. HAZARD — not walkable. Use for lava rivers and pools. Strips 2-5 tiles for rivers, patches 5-15 for pools. Never place next to snow or water tiles.

volcanic-magma-crack.name: Volcanic Magma Crack
volcanic-magma-crack.description: Dark rock with bright orange magma visible through cracks. Use near lava features as transition. Strips 1-3 tiles around lava.

volcanic-molten.name: Volcanic Molten Rock
volcanic-molten.description: Semi-molten rock glowing with heat. HAZARD — not walkable. Use as transition ring around lava. 1-2 tiles between lava and solid ground.

volcanic-obsidian.name: Volcanic Obsidian
volcanic-obsidian.description: Smooth, dark obsidian glass. Use for volcanic glass formations and obsidian fields. Patches of 5-15 tiles.

volcanic-pumice.name: Volcanic Pumice
volcanic-pumice.description: Light, porous pumice stone surface. Use near cooled lava flows. Patches of 5-12 tiles.

volcanic-rock.name: Volcanic Rock
volcanic-rock.description: Standard dark volcanic rock. Use as base ground in volcanic areas. Large patches 15-30 tiles.

volcanic-scorched.name: Volcanic Scorched Earth
volcanic-scorched.description: Heat-scorched golden-brown earth near volcanic activity. Use as transition between volcanic and desert/temperate zones. Strips 3-6 tiles.

volcanic-sulfur.name: Volcanic Sulfur
volcanic-sulfur.description: Yellow sulfur deposits around volcanic vents. Use sparingly near geothermal features. Small patches 2-6 tiles.

water-calm.name: Calm Water
water-calm.description: Still, calm water surface. Use for ponds and sheltered lake areas. Patches of 10-30 tiles. Not walkable.

water-crystal.name: Crystal Clear Water
water-crystal.description: Very clear, transparent water. Use for mountain springs and pristine pools. Small patches 5-15 tiles. Not walkable.

water-deep.name: Deep Water
water-deep.description: Dark, deep water. Use for ocean depths, deep lakes, and rivers. Large areas 20+ tiles for open water. Not walkable.

water-foam.name: Foamy Water
water-foam.description: Water with white foam on the surface. Use at rapids, waterfall bases, and wave-crashing shores. Strips 1-3 tiles. Not walkable.

water-murky.name: Murky Water
water-murky.description: Dark, murky, opaque water. Use for swamp pools, polluted areas, and stagnant water. Patches of 5-20 tiles. Not walkable.

water-ripple.name: Rippling Water
water-ripple.description: Water with gentle ripples. Use for lakes and calm rivers. Patches of 10-25 tiles. Not walkable.

water-river.name: River Water
water-river.description: Flowing river water with slight current pattern and reeds at edges. Use for rivers, 2-5 tiles wide in snaking patterns. Not walkable.

water-shallow.name: Shallow Water Alt
water-shallow.description: Alternative shallow water tile. Use interchangeably with shallow-water for transition zones. Not walkable.

water-stormy.name: Stormy Water
water-stormy.description: Rough, dark stormy water with whitecaps. Use for ocean areas and storm-tossed seas. Large patches 20+ tiles. Not walkable.

water-turquoise.name: Turquoise Water
water-turquoise.description: Beautiful turquoise tropical water. Use for tropical lagoons and coral reef areas. Patches of 10-25 tiles. Not walkable.

wet-mud-puddles.name: Wet Mud Puddles
wet-mud-puddles.description: Muddy ground with small puddles. Use for rainy areas and swamp edges. Sparse patches 2-8 tiles.

white-marble.name: White Marble
white-marble.description: Polished white marble surface. Use for palace floors, temple steps, and wealthy building exteriors. Patches of 8-25 tiles.

white-sand-beach.name: White Sand Beach
white-sand-beach.description: Pristine white tropical sand. Use interchangeably with beach-white for tropical beach areas. Large patches 20+ tiles.

wood-chips.name: Wood Chips
wood-chips.description: Ground covered in wood chips and shavings. Use near lumber mills, carpenter shops, and forest-stumps areas. Small patches 3-8 tiles.

wooden-planks.name: Wooden Planks
wooden-planks.description: Rough wooden plank surface. Use for docks, bridges, outdoor platforms, and simple building floors. Rectangular areas matching the structure.

worn-stone-path.name: Worn Stone Path
worn-stone-path.description: Old, worn stone pathway. Use for ancient paths, temple approaches, and old road remnants. Strips 1-3 tiles wide.

woven-carpet.name: Woven Carpet
woven-carpet.description: Simple woven carpet/rug. Use for basic indoor rooms and merchant tents. Small areas 3-10 tiles.
